rationale: In the conceptual framework, the citing paper uses Okada (2005) as direct support for a key mechanism: consumers are more willing to choose hedonic options when those choices are easier to justify. That claim is then used to motivate the authors' argument that SNAP shoppers may underestimate unhealthiness to rationalize hedonic food purchases. Even though the citation appears in a grouped parenthetical and is not methodologically reused, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in hypothesis development.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Okada (2005) as prior justification-based evidence for hedonic versus utilitarian choice, and uses it to ground its mechanism that justification processes can increase hedonic food purchasing. This serves as conceptual support for the paper's proposed craving-to-justification pathway.
